key_insight: Chemical short-range order (CSRO) in Co30Cr40Ni30 raises stacking-fault energetics and suppresses fcc→hcp deformation pathways, making atomic-scale ordering a key control knob for mechanical behavior.
engineering_relevance: By tuning CSRO through heat treatment, alloy designers can steer deformation mechanisms and improve control over strength–ductility–transformation balance in complex concentrated alloys.
method: Compared quenched (CSRO-lean) and aged (CSRO-enriched) alloys with matched grain structure and phase constitution.
Used calorimetry, synchrotron X-ray diffraction, and electron microscopy to track CSRO and deformation pathways.
Validated the mechanism with atomistic simulations of stacking-fault energetics.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports reversible CSRO formation and consistent suppression of deformation-induced fcc-hcp martensitic transformation in experiments, supported by simulations showing higher stable and unstable stacking-fault energies with CSRO.
watch_item: The conclusions are demonstrated for one Co30Cr40Ni30 composition, so broader applicability across other CCAs may require composition-specific validation.
paper_title: Chemical short-range order controls deformation pathways in a complex concentrated alloy
